title: Supergroups (Large-Scale Group Conversations)
description: Supergroups are high-capacity group conversations (up to 200,000 members) with persistent message history, searchable archive, granular admin permissions, topics/threads, and anonymous admin posting. Regular groups have a hard cap of 200 members and no persistent history by default. Upgrade path: regular group → supergroup is one-way and irreversible.

regular_group.message_history: visible only to members present at time of message

supergroup.message_history: fully persistent, visible to all new joiners from beginning
supergroup.invite_links: multiple custom links with per-link limits and expiry

server_steps: 2. Verify caller is owner (cannot be delegated)
6. Backfill: UPDATE all existing messages SET visible_to_new_members = TRUE
7. Emit domain event: group.upgraded (for fanout to all members via WebSocket)
9. Purge group config from all CDN/cache layers
upgrade_protocol.irreversibility_rationale: Downgrade would require hiding history from members who joined after upgrade — violates privacy contract. Architecturally prevented at DB level (no downgrade API).
upgrade_protocol.failure_rollback: If any step 1-8 fails: full ROLLBACK. Client receives 503 with retry guidance.

anonymous_admin.description: Admin posts appear as group name, not personal account. Server replaces sender_id with a sentinel group_actor_id in message payload to recipients.
anonymous_admin.security: True sender_id stored in messages_admin_audit table (service_role only). Legal hold compliant.

topics_threads.description: Sub-conversations within a supergroup. Each topic has id, name, icon_color, is_general (exactly one per group).

scalability.member_list_pagination: Cursor-based, page size 100. No COUNT(*) queries — use estimated counts via pg_stats.
scalability.message_fan_out: For groups > 10,000 members: switch from direct WebSocket push to pub/sub topic per group_id. Clients subscribe on join.
scalability.write_amplification: Supergroup message write = 1 message row + 1 pub/sub event. No per-member write. Recipients pull via WebSocket subscription.
